reports.text:
ARVIND ADVANCED MATERIALS ACQUIRES U.S.-BASED DALCO-GFT 🇺🇸🧵

• 🌍 Arvind Advanced Materials (AAML) to acquire ~61% controlling stake in U.S.-based Dalco-GFT, entering the world’s largest technical textile market

• 💰 Dalco-GFT is a ~$100 Mn revenue company with ~17% EBITDA margin and ~40% ROCE

• 🏭 Dalco-GFT specializes in needle-punched non-woven technical textiles serving automotive, industrial, construction & furnishing sectors

• 🇺🇸 Acquisition provides access to ~$2.5 Bn U.S. technical textile TAM and expands AAML’s global manufacturing footprint

• 🔗 Deal expected to enhance supply-chain resilience, cross-selling opportunities and customer diversification

• 📈 Acquisition is both margin and EPS accretive from year one; AAML plans to acquire remaining stake over 4 years

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic U.S. expansion strengthens Arvind’s global technical textile positioning and earnings profile)

Total Insurance Premium for the full year was 29,934 Cr, up 42% YoY, led by growth in core online new protection business at 57% YoY (new Health insurance up 68% YoY). Quarterly Insurance Premium was at *9,217 Cr, up 46% YoY again led by growth in core online new protection business at 67% YoY.

 FY26 Consolidated operating revenue grew 37% YoY to ₹6,794 Cr (Core Insurance revenue up 41% YoY); Quarterly Consolidated Operating Revenue grew 37% to ₹2,061 Cr (Q4FY26 Core Insurance revenue up 48% YoY; Q4FY26 Core credit revenue, up 7% YoY).

Our core renewal / trail revenue on a 12-month rolling basis is at ₹935 Cr, up from 668 Cr last year same quarter, a 40% growth led by growth of 55% in the insurance segment. The quarterly core insurance renewal revenue is at an ARR of ₹1126 Cr, up from 689 Cr Q4 last year (growth of 63% YoY). This is a key driver of long-term profit growth.

 Growth accelerated for Q4FY26 Core New Insurance Premium (net of Savings business) at 59% YoY. Core New Insurance Premium (Including Savings) grew 48% YoY for the quarter. Excluding Savings category, we have been growing between 34% to 59% for the last 12 quarters.

We continue to improve our customer onboarding & claims support services and Insurance CSAT is consistent above 90%

Outlook
- Management expects high single digit industry growth in FY27  
- Targeting to outperform industry growth  
- EBITDA margin guidance maintained at 14 to 16%  
- Around 2% price hike implemented in April to offset commodity inflation  

Final Takeaway
- Strong growth momentum across scooters, EVs and exports with aggressive investments supporting long term expansion  

Impact
- Positive

• 🛠️ Company scheduled annual maintenance shutdown at its Rehana Jattan, Phagwara (Punjab) manufacturing unit from May 15, 2026

• ⏳ Plant expected to remain non-operational for around 10 days from the shutdown date

• 🌽 Unit manufactures Maize Starch, Dextrose Monohydrate, Sorbitol, Dextrose Anhydrous and by-products

• 📊 Facility contributes nearly 35% of company’s total maize grind capacity and around 40% of overall sales

• 🔄 Shutdown is part of routine annual maintenance activities

🟠 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Negative (Temporary production halt may impact short-term volumes, though planned maintenance is routine in nature)

POLYCAB Q4 ; CO DELIVERS 27% YoY REVENUE GROWTH IN Q4; W&C & FMEG LEAD PERFORMANCE ⚡📈

• 💰 Consolidated revenue rose 27% YoY to ₹8,865 Cr, driven by strong execution across Wires & Cables (W&C) and FMEG businesses

• 🔌 W&C segment revenue grew 30% YoY, led by strong domestic demand and execution under “Project Spring”; company gained ~3–4% market share during FY26

• 🌍 International business grew 18% YoY, contributing 4.4% to consolidated revenue

• 📉 W&C margins declined YoY due to higher institutional sales mix and operating deleverage; EBITDA margin remained within guided 12%–14% range

• ☀️ FMEG business posted strong 47% YoY growth; solar products nearly doubled YoY and emerged as the largest category in the portfolio

• 📈 FMEG profitability improved despite higher brand investments, aided by operating leverage and better product mix

• 🏗️ EPC business revenue declined 15% YoY due to project execution timing; EBIT margin stood at 7.6%

reports.text:
CG Power - Q4 FY26 Result Analysis

Q4 Revenue: ₹3,442 cr · +25.0% YoY · +8.4% QoQ 
Q4 EBITDA: ₹466 cr · 13.6% margin · +95 bps YoY 
Q4 PAT (owners): ₹365 cr · +34.4% YoY FY26 
Normalised PAT: ₹1,232 cr · +26.3% YoY

PAT grew faster than revenue. EBITDA margins expanded. The core business is in a structural upcycle and it is showing up cleanly in the numbers.

Power Systems is the story. The numbers are exceptional.

Q4 Power Systems revenue: ₹1,487 cr · +49.7% YoY. PBIT: ₹354 cr · +60.6% YoY. PBIT margin: 23.8% · +287 bps YoY.

For the full year, Power Systems grew revenue 46.4% to ₹5,138 cr and now represents 41% of consolidated revenue. The standalone order book stood at ₹14,859 cr at December-end with Q4 executing strongly on it. Near-full capacity utilisation is already the base, and CWIP grew 82% YoY to ₹647 cr as the next tranche of transformer capacity is being built.

This is not a company benefiting from a one-year capex spike. The power transmission infrastructure cycle in India is a multi-year programme and CG Power is executing inside it at scale.

The standalone entity tells you where the real profit lives.

Q4 standalone PAT: ₹412 cr · +49.4% YoY. Consolidated PAT attributable to owners: ₹365 cr. The ₹47 cr gap is almost entirely the Semiconductor segment's quarterly PBIT loss of ₹37 cr. For the full year, the consolidated-standalone PAT gap is ₹111 cr, again matching the full-year Semiconductor PBIT loss of ₹108 cr.

The core Indian Power and Industrial business is compounding at 35%+ PAT growth. The drag comes entirely from a new business that is deliberately in investment mode.

Industrial Systems is the weak link but it's stabilising.

Q4 Industrial Systems revenue: ₹1,791 cr · +2.3% YoY. PBIT: ₹160 cr · −16.5% YoY. PBIT margin: 8.95% · −202 bps YoY.

The railway business within this segment continues to weigh on profitability. Management guided that recovery would take a few more quarters, Q4 shows the margin stabilising sequentially from Q3's 9.02% to 9.0% in Q4, but the YoY comparison remains ugly. Industrial Systems is 52% of Q4 revenue and 33% of PBIT, it matters, and it needs to recover above 10% margins for the blended picture to improve.

The balance sheet is the strongest it has ever been.

Post-QIP equity: ₹8,198 cr · +103% YoY. Borrowings: ₹0.26 cr. Effectively debt-free. ₹3,000 cr raised via QIP, ₹619 cr utilised so far, the remaining proceeds are parked in FDs and mutual funds and will be deployed into capex as the transformer and semiconductor facilities scale.

One flag: CFO declined 23% YoY to ₹702 cr despite higher profits. Trade receivables grew ₹912 cr and inventory grew ₹409 cr as the business scaled rapidly. CFO/normalised PAT at 0.57x is low. This is a working capital cycle question, not an earnings quality question but it bears watching as revenue continues to grow.

Semiconductor: the long-duration bet.

FY26 Semiconductor revenue: ₹503 cr. PBIT loss: ₹108 cr. Government grant receivables: ₹668 cr, ₹601 cr already received. This is a business being built over a multi-year horizon with significant government support. The losses are budgeted and expected. The question for investors is whether they're comfortable with a ₹100+ cr annual drag for the next 2–3 years while the segment matures.

Note : This is not an investment advise.

• 🧪 New study by a premier Govt-linked technical institution found PLA-based bioplastics degrade naturally in Indian soil without leaving harmful microplastics

• 📉 PLA microplastic particles reduced sharply from 287 particles/kg to just 18 particles/kg within 180 days

• 🌍 Conventional petroleum-based plastics showed almost no reduction even after 6 months, remaining persistent in soil

• 🌾 Study highlighted no toxic heavy metal residue from PLA degradation, making it safer for soil and agriculture

• 🪱 Earthworms exposed to PLA showed healthy biological activity, supporting soil ecosystem compatibility

• 🇮🇳 Findings strengthen the case for compostable polymers and sustainable plastic alternatives in India

🟢 Impact: Positive (Supports long-term growth outlook for biodegradable & compostable materials ecosystem)

Continuation of these programs would have resulted in 4%-5% sequential growth.

Solid growth is expected from Commercial (On & Off-Highway), USA, India, China. Connected Vehicle, Autonomous Driving & Aftersales Transformation. Growth acceleration seen in H2 led by Products and Solutions.

EBITDA: 20.5-21.2% post Increased Investments in Al, Products & Solutions, Competency Development and New Markets

reports.text:
📢 Digikore Studios Delivers Blockbuster FY26 Results
_FY26 Total Income Surges 91.5% to ₹70.86 Crore; PAT Margin Expands to 17.83%, PAT Reaches ₹12.64 Crore; Net Worth Rises 37.1% to ₹47.68 Crore_

📌Key Highlights:
* Revenue from Operations at ₹66.02 Cr (+83.1% YoY)
* Total Revenue at ₹70.86 Cr (+91.5% YoY)
* PAT at ₹12.64 Cr vs Loss of ₹7.20 Cr in FY25
* PBT at ₹15.37 Cr vs Loss of ₹9.61 Cr in FY25
* PAT Margin at 17.8% vs negative margin in FY25
* EPS improved to ₹9.98  vs (-₹11.37)
* Balance Sheet: Net Worth rose 37.1% to ₹47.68 Cr

📌Operational Efficiency
* Employee Benefit Expense down 13.4% YoY
* Total Expenses increased only 19.0% despite 91.5% revenue growth
* Strong operating leverage reflected in margins

📌Strategic / Business Update
* Performance driven by Project Abhimanyu turnaround initiative
* Focused on stronger execution, cashflow quality, and financial resilience
* Better positioned for larger global VFX mandates
* Beneficiary of improving global VFX demand environment

📌Overall Takeaway:
Digikore delivered a sharp turnaround in FY26 with strong revenue growth, return to profitability, margin expansion, and improved balance sheet strength—indicating successful early execution of its strategic restructuring program.

As the global VFX industry improves and our US and European business development efforts continue to gain traction, we believe the Company is now better positioned to pursue larger opportunities, scale with greater confidence, and create long-term value for shareholders.

reports.text:
SEIL Receives 5-Year Renewal of MES Approval

Steel Exchange India Ltd. has received renewal of approval from the Military Engineer Services (MES) under the Ministry of Defence for supply of SIMHADRI TMT bars for the next 5 years.

Update Highlights:
* Approval renewed for Fe 500D and Fe 500D HCRM TMT bars from 8 mm to 32 mm.
* Products are manufactured at SEIL’s integrated Vizianagaram plant using TEMPCORE technology.
* Renewal follows MES quality checks, technical evaluations, and inspection standards.
* MES approvals are granted only to manufacturers meeting stringent quality requirements.
* Approval remains subject to periodic inspections and IS 1786:2008 compliance.
* SEIL continues to remain eligible for MES-linked infrastructure and defence projects.

Strategic Alignment:
The renewal strengthens SEIL’s position in institutional and government-linked segments while reinforcing its focus on quality standards, operational discipline, and participation in high entry-barrier projects.

reports.text:
Thailand's Land Bridge: A Game-Changer for Global Trade?

Thailand is pushing ahead with its ambitious $30B+ Land Bridge — a 90km multimodal corridor linking the Andaman Sea to the Gulf of Thailand. The goal? Create a faster, cheaper overland shortcut that bypasses the congested and vulnerable Strait of Malacca, shaving 4-5 days off shipping times and ~15% off costs.

In an era of Red Sea/Hormuz disruptions and growing worries about maritime chokepoints, this project isn't just infrastructure — it's strategic resilience. If executed well, it could reposition Thailand as a key logistics hub in the Indo-Pacific, challenging Singapore's dominance and reshaping supply chains.

Decades in discussion, now gaining real momentum. The big questions remain: execution, environment, and geopolitics. Worth watching closely.

Politicians: The Demographic of Disconnect

While the common man pours into SIPs, India’s political class remains allergic to the demat account. Out of ₹29,032 crore in disclosed assets across five states, a staggering 99% is locked in land, buildings, and gold. Only a minuscule 0.97% sits in regulated capital markets—a stark contrast to US Congress members who often hold 50-80% of their wealth in stocks.

The data reveals a "sideline" mentality: only 461 out of 7,325 candidates disclosed any market exposure. For a leadership pushing a "Viksit Bharat" built on financialization, their personal portfolios tell a different story. They aren't betting on India’s corporate growth; they are betting on the soil beneath it. The biggest underserved market for financial advisors? The very people making the policies.

• 🏛️ Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) approves development of state-of-the-art Ship Repair Facility at Vadinar, Gujarat on May 5, 2026 — jointly implemented by Deendayal Port Authority (DPA) & Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL)

• 💰 Total project investment: ₹1,570 Cr | DPA's share: ₹650 Cr (civil infrastructure & 650m jetty) | CSL's share: ₹920 Cr (two large floating dry docks & facility operations) | Mode: Internal resources + debt | Timeline: 36 months

• 🚢 Capacity: Will enable repair of vessels up to 300 m length — vs CSL's existing facilities which handle up to 250 m; addresses India's critical gap in repairing large commercial & foreign-flagged vessels

• 📍 Location advantage: Vadinar's deep draft & proximity to Mundra and Kandla ports — strategic position on India's western coast

• 👷 Employment: ~290 direct + ~1,100 indirect jobs to be created | Aligns with Maritime India Vision 2030 & Amrit Kaal Vision 2047

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major government-backed capacity expansion for CSL — high-value ship repair market entry, reduces India's dependence on foreign shipyards & adds significant long-term revenue potential for Cochin Shipyard)

ADANI GREEN , OTHER ADANI STOCKS : ADANI SEC CASE MAY HEAD TOWARDS NEGOTIATED US SETTLEMENT : Business World Report 

• 🏛️ Senior legal & policy sources in Washington indicate the long-running U.S. legal overhang involving Gautam Adani could be resolved through a negotiated settlement instead of a prolonged courtroom battle

• 📜 Discussions around a potential settlement framework have reportedly intensified in recent weeks despite the case continuing procedurally in the Eastern District of New York

• ⚖️ Proposed structure may involve a civil settlement “without admitting or denying” allegations — a commonly used mechanism in complex SEC and DOJ enforcement matters

• 🇺🇸 Sources suggest enforcement circles within the U.S. Department of Justice and SEC increasingly view a negotiated closure as the most practical path forward

• ⏳ Settlement agreement is reportedly “around the corner” and could be announced this month, according to Washington-based sources

• 🌍 Such a resolution, if finalized, could significantly reduce legal uncertainty surrounding the Adani Group’s global fundraising and expansion plans

🟢 Impact: Positive (Potential settlement could remove a major overhang on Adani Group sentiment, funding access and international investor confidence)

– Fixed stationary systems

Jammu & Kashmir Bank: Guidance  - Concall Update

For FY26-27, the bank has set conservative guidance: credit growth of 12%, deposit growth of 10%, CASA at 45%, NIM around 3.5%, ROA maintained around current levels (1.37%), ROE around 16%, and gross NPA below 2.25%.

The MD and CEO aspires for the bank to cross INR5 lakh crores in total business within three years, implying a growth rate of around 17-18%.

Employee costs are expected to continue to decline, with the quarterly run rate around INR500 crore for the next two quarters and the full-year cost contained below INR2,500 crore, despite potential new hires.

Credit cost is projected to be around 0.1% or 0.2% maximum for the next year, not factoring in the impact of ECL provisions.

 The 3.5% NIM guidance is conservative due to the lingering impact of last year's difficulties in Jammu and Kashmir affecting retail advances, but an upward trend is expected, with potential for revision next quarter.

OSWAL PUMPS SECURES ₹162 CR SOLAR PUMP ORDER FROM MSEDCL ☀️🚰

• 🏭 Company received empanelment order from Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (MSEDCL) for 6,896 Off-Grid DC Solar Photovoltaic Water Pumping Systems (SPWPS)

• 💰 Total order value stands at approximately ₹162.06 Cr (including GST)

• 🌱 Management said the project will help reduce dependence on diesel-based irrigation and support renewable energy adoption in agriculture

• 📈 Company highlighted the order strengthens its position as a preferred partner for large-scale solar water pumping solutions

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong order inflow enhances revenue visibility and strengthens renewable energy & agriculture sector presence)

Transformer industry trends from concalls and PPTs post results

1. Lead times have increased for Bushings and insulators.

2. Transformer oil prices have increased. Transformer oil is 8-12% of overall costs. Key suppliers are the likes of APAR and Savita oil.

3. US tariffs have come down from 50% to 25% to finally 10%. 

4. Middle East exports have impacted sales. 

5. Backlogs are bigger for Power transformers vs IDT or distribution transformers at the moment.

Near term margins can be weak due to the reasons above inspite of a healthy order book & enquiry pipeline.

TRANSFORMER SPACE – CURRENT REALITY

What’s Happening
- Stocks correcting after results  
- Margin pressure across companies  
- Industry facing input cost issues  
- Short-term weakness visible  

Key Issues
- Raw material costs increasing  
- Shortage of critical components  
- Bushings supply constraints  
- Cost pressures impacting margins  

Margin Impact
- Industry-wide margin compression  
- Profitability under near-term pressure  
- Execution becoming more important

Voltamp Transformers - Q4 FY26 Result Analysis

Q4 Revenue: ₹617 cr · −1.2% YoY · −2.1% QoQ 
Q4 Normalised EBITDA margin: 9.27% · −1,024 bps YoY 
Q4 Normalised PAT: ₹68 cr · −32.3% YoY 
Q4 Reported PAT: ₹48 cr · −50.5% YoY

The reported PAT is dragged further by a ₹24 cr MTM loss on financial instruments sitting in Other Income. Strip that and normalised PAT is still down 32%. The MTM is not the story, the material cost explosion is.
Material costs hit 84.6% of Q4 sales. That is the only number that matters.

FY25 full year: 71.2%. FY26 full year: 78.0%. Q4 FY26 alone: 84.6%.

The direction is unambiguous and the Q4 number is alarming. Copper and CRGO (cold-rolled grain-oriented silicon steel) - the two primary inputs for transformer manufacturing, saw sharp price increases. Revenue was flat to slightly down in Q4. Material costs grew 38.7% YoY. The result was a normalised EBITDA margin of 9.27% in Q4 versus 19.51% a year ago, a 1,024 bps collapse.

This is not a company that chose lower margins to win volume. Revenue actually declined. Pricing power simply wasn't there to pass through cost inflation.

FY26 full year: the revenue story was fine, the margin story was not.

FY26 revenue: ₹2,154 cr · +11.4% YoY. Strong order book execution, high capacity utilisation, real demand for transformers from power infrastructure spending. The top line held.

FY26 normalised EBITDA: 17.25% · down 359 bps YoY from 20.84%. FY26 normalised PAT: ₹296 cr · −8.8% YoY. Management had flagged "margin normalisation" as a risk. The actual outcome was significantly more severe than normalization implies. At 9.27% in Q4, you are not normalising, you are at a level where the business is barely covering its costs of production.

Cash flow is the other concern.

FY26 CFO: ₹140 cr · down 36% YoY. CFO/PAT: 0.46x - weak earnings quality by any measure. The primary culprit: inventory ballooned 64% YoY to ₹390 cr, absorbing ₹152 cr of operating cash. The company was likely stocking raw materials ahead of further price rises, a rational response to volatile input costs but one that hits cash conversion hard.

FCF: ~₹15 cr after ₹125 cr of capex for the new greenfield EHV plant. Barely positive.

The balance sheet remains pristine - debt-free, equity at 89% of total assets, ₹967 cr in investments. Dividend of ₹100/share maintained. The financial strength is real. The operational pressures are also real.

The EHV greenfield is the FY27 and FY28 lever.

CWIP surged from ₹3.4 cr to ₹108 cr during FY26, the new Extra High Voltage transformer facility in Jarod is on track for June 2026 commissioning. EHV transformers command better margins and expand the addressable market significantly. An additional ₹25 cr land acquisition near Vadodara has been approved for future expansion.

If copper prices stabilise and the EHV plant ramps, FY27 could look materially different from Q4 FY26. If input costs stay elevated and pricing remains competitive, the margin pressure persists.

Note : This is not an investment advise.

Steep increase in critical components cost, with vendors chasing export markets and passing on their increased cost burden.

Shareholding Pattern - Sector/Cap wise quarterly changes for Mar'26

Recode Studios: The outlier in the beauty space 

• Trades at just 14x P/E
• EBITDA margin at 23%+
• RoE at 68%

👉 
Lower valuation. 
Higher profitability. 
Stronger returns.

Peers comparison:
Honasa → 71x P/E | 3.3% margin
Nykaa → 502x P/E | 6% margin
Ravelcare → 25x P/E | 27% margin
Recode → 14x P/E | 23% margin

Big takeaway:
You rarely find low valuation + high profitability + strong returns in one stock. Recode fits all three.

🚀 **CRYOGENIC OGS: SMALL BASE, STRONG EXECUTION STORY

📊 H2FY26 vs H2FY**25
• Revenue: ₹19.76 Cr | ▲ 1.37%
• EBITDA: ₹5.67 Cr | ▲ 29.57%
• EBITDA Margin: 28.68% vs 22.44%
• PAT: ₹4.67 Cr | ▲ 41.15%

**👉 Key Takeaway**
Margins and profitability are scaling *significantly faster* than revenue highlighting strong operating leverage and execution efficiency.

**🔥 Key Growth Drivers**
• EIL approval unlocking PSU tenders and large LNG and refinery opportunities
• ₹8 Cr+ domestic inflows + $231K export orders improving revenue visibility
• Strategic engagements with Emerson and Honeywell
• Early integration into global supply chains

**💡 What Stands Out**
• Strong margin expansion despite modest revenue growth
• Debt-free balance sheet
• High entry barriers business
• Promoter accumulation trend

NOMURA ON FEDERAL BANK

Upgrade from Neutral to Buy with a target price of ₹330 (earlier ₹215)

4QFY26 strong, with consistent execution supporting upgrade

Expect ~17% EPS CAGR over FY26-28F, with RoA/RoE at ~1.3%/12% by FY28F

Asset quality remains strong, better than edin mid-tier peers

Core PPOP beat, led by fee income and controlled costs

Healthy loan growth in focus segments, with stable asset quality

GOLDMAN SACHS ON INDIA TELECOM/INDUS TOWERS

Maintain Sell on Indus Towers, Target price unchanged at 365

Core organic revenue and EBITDA growth slowed to 5% YoY in 4QFY26

Brokerage expects growth moderation with Bharti tower rollouts nearing completion

FY27 revenue/EBITDA growth estimated at 5%/7%

Vodafone Idea AGR relief could support incremental tower rollout activity

Highlights potential risks from Africa expansion and customer contract expiry

MORGAN STANLEY ON MANAPPURAM FINANCE

Maintain Equal-weight , Target price unchanged at ₹ 270

Asirvad MFI posted adjusted Q4 profit of 135 mn versus estimate of loss

Lower provisions and ECL model changes aided profitability

GNPA declined to 4.85% while provision coverage improved to 68%

Rights issue strengthened capital adequacy to 31.4%

Awaits outlook on provisions, growth and funding availability

MORGAN STANLEY ON CREDIT GROWTH TRENDS

Industry View: Attractive

Non-food credit growth improved to 15.9% YoY in March 2026

Retail, services, industry and agri segments all saw acceleration

MSME loan growth remained strong at 29.6% YoY

NBFC lending growth accelerated to 26.3% YoY

Gold loan growth remained elevated at 123% YoY

Fortnightly April data indicates moderation in momentum

**Target Price: ₹1,300**

• Revenue/EBITDA grew ~16.6%/10.5% YoY

• EBITDA margin at ~26.6% YoY  
• Pricing strategy remains calibrated  
• FY27 growth guided at 13–15%  
• Margins expected stable at ~27–28%

**Nomura on Dr Lal PathLabs**

• Q4FY26 growth accelerating across key metrics

• Commodity tailwinds supporting margins  
• Operating leverage aiding profitability  
• FMEG segment turnaround underway  
• ~21% EPS CAGR expected over FY26–29

**HSBC on Aster DM Healthcare**

**Target Price: ₹785**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Improving case mix and expansion to drive growth  

• Q4FY26 strong, led by better case mix  
• Further scope to improve patient mix  
• Retaining top clinical talent remains key  
• Expansion in key markets like Bengaluru  
• Aster-QCIL merger expected in Q1FY27

IEX real-time power price falls to ₹0.0003/unit on Apr 5 amid solar oversupply; swings between ₹0.40 and ₹10/unit in April. Exchanges handle 12–13% of supply; ~5–6 GW curtailed with 11.5 GW cut in Rajasthan since Jan; storage at 1,082 MWh vs 236.22 GWh target by FY32.

**Jefferies on Adani Ports**

**Target Price: ₹1,980**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong realisations and long-term growth drivers  

• Q4 EBITDA beat by ~9%  
• Domestic port realisations drove performance  
• FY27 EBITDA growth guided at 9–14%  
• Growth driven by volumes and capacity expansion  
• Industrialisation and trade support demand  
• ROCE to expand ~1% annually

**Goldman Sachs on Adani Ports**

**Target Price: ₹1,710**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Logistics growth driving overall performance  

• Revenue broadly in-line with estimates  
• Logistics and marine drove growth strongly  
• Port cargo volumes up ~13% YoY  
• Port margins at ~66.4%  
• Logistics margins improved ~250 bps YoY  
• FY27 revenue growth guided at 11–16%

**Nomura on Adani Ports**

**Target Price: ₹1,930**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Robust outlook with capacity expansion plans  

• Q4 EBITDA beat estimates by ~6%  
• FY27 EBITDA growth guided at 9–14%  
• FY28 EBITDA estimates raised by ~6%  
• Targets ~19% EBITDA CAGR FY26–28  
• Capacity to reach ~1,000 MTPA by CY30  
• Trades at ~13x FY28 EV/EBITDA

**HSBC on Adani Ports**

**Target Price: ₹1,950 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Strong growth visibility despite near-term caution  

• Q4 EBITDA grew ~20% YoY, beat estimates  
• FY27 guidance cautious amid Middle East disruptions  
• Targets ~18% EBITDA CAGR till FY31  
• ROCE expected around ~20% by FY31  
• ~$9 bn headroom available for M&A  
• TP raised on improved medium-term outlook

**Jefferies on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹450**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Healthy growth with improving credit trends  

• Q4 PAT ~₹40 bn, up ~13% YoY  
• Loan growth strong at ~16% YoY  
• NII grew ~8% YoY  
• CASA growth healthy at ~16%  
• Credit quality improving across segments  
• Earnings trimmed on lower NIM guidance

**Nomura on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹460**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Asset quality improvement driving earnings upgrades  

• Strong NIM performance in Q4  
• Asset quality improved sharply  
• FY27–28 EPS raised by ~2%  
• Lower credit cost and opex support growth  
• RoA/RoE seen at ~2%/12% by FY28  
• EPS CAGR estimated at ~18%

**JP Morgan on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹476**  
**Recommendation: Overweight**  

Strong core performance with steady margins  

• Q4 PAT ₹40.3 bn, up ~13% YoY  
• NII beat estimates; strong core performance  
• Opex lower and provisions declined sharply  
• Non-interest income remained muted YoY  
• NII grew ~8.1% YoY, among best peers  
• Core NIM stable; reported NIM up ~13 bps

**UBS on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹500**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Improving profitability with favourable risk-reward  

• PAT beat driven by higher PPOP  
• Lower credit cost supported earnings  
• Loan/deposit growth at 3.2%/5.5% QoQ  
• CASA ratio improved by ~200 bps QoQ  
• ECL transition impact below 2% net worth  
• Attractive valuation at ~1.5x FY27E P/BV

**Bernstein on Kotak Bank**

**Target Price: ₹500**  
**Recommendation: Market Perform**  

Strong operating performance but RoE still lags  

• Beat across most operating metrics in Q4  
• NIM expanded ~13 bps QoQ, peer-leading  
• Credit cost normalized to ~39 bps  
• Loan growth steady at ~16% YoY  
• Stable opex supported RoA above 2%  
• RoE at ~12.3%, below large peers

**DAM Capital on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹434**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

Growth moderate amid capex and tenancy concerns  

• Financials broadly in-line with estimates  
• Tower additions higher than estimates  
• Incremental tenancies relatively muted  
• Maintenance capex remains elevated  
• ARPT flat, key disappointment  
• Revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR at 8/6/6%  
• Valuation multiple cut to 7.0x

**Jefferies on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹370**  
**Recommendation: Underperform**  

Limited triggers with modest growth outlook  

• Q4 broadly in-line operationally  
• Dividend ₹14/share below expectations  
• EPS CAGR seen at ~3% FY26–29  
• Dividend yield below 4%  
• AGR clarity already priced in  
• Upcoming renewal risks remain concern

**HSBC on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹345**  
**Recommendation: Reduce**  

Weak FCF outlook and pricing pressure concerns  

• FCF and dividends below estimates  
• Capex increased ~28% YoY  
• Organic co-location additions declined YoY  
• Pricing power remains limited  
• Rentals likely to stay under pressure  
• Slower FCF growth key de-rating risk

**UBS on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹495**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

Mixed operational trends with capex concerns  

• Q4 results slightly soft vs expectations  
• Tower additions improved to ~4.9k  
• Tenancy additions strong at ~6.2k  
• Tenancy ratio flat at ~1.62x  
• Rentals declined QoQ across metrics  
• Capex increased to ₹23.3 bn  
• FY26 capex up ~28% YoY  
• Dividend ₹14/share below expectations

**Nomura on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹505 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Stable performance with catalysts ahead  

• Q4 stable; tenancy additions strong  
• Dividend reinstatement positive signal  
• Lower Vi AGR may aid fundraising  
• EBITDA dipped QoQ due to maintenance  
• Trades at ~6.3x FY28 EV/EBITDA  
• Vi debt raise progress key catalyst

**Citi on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹530**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

Dividend return and tenancy growth key positives  

• Dividend resumed after four-year gap  
• ₹14/share equals ~100% FY26 FCF  
• Implies sustainable 4–5% yield outlook  
• Financial performance broadly in-line  
• Strong tenancy additions from Vodafone Idea  
• Higher contribution from Bharti Airtel  
• AGR clarity positive for business outlook

**CLSA on Indus Towers**

**Target Price: ₹580**  
**Recommendation: Outperform (High Conviction)**  

Strong growth outlook with improving sector tailwinds  

• Q4 revenue ₹53 bn, in-line estimates  
• EBITDA up 6% YoY, flat QoQ  
• Record tenancy additions at ~6.2k  
• Added ~4.9k towers in Q4FY26  
• FY26 EBITDA grew ~11% YoY  
• AGR relief for Vodafone Idea supportive  
• Dividend reinstated at ₹14/share  
• Net cash position at ₹49 bn  
• Valuation attractive at ~6x EV/EBITDA

**UBS on Vodafone Idea**

**Target Price: ₹12.40**  
**Recommendation: Neutral**  

AGR relief supports valuation but risks remain  

• AGR dues revised to ₹640 bn  
• Payment schedule starts from FY32  
• Nominal payments in early years  
• Bulk repayment over FY36–41  
• Potential ~20% equity value upside  
• Funding and execution remain key monitor
APL Apollo to increase structural steel capacity to 8 MTPA from 5 MTPA

**Citi on Vodafone Idea**

**Target Price: ₹14**  
**Recommendation: Buy (High Risk)**  

Regulatory clarity improves financial viability  

• AGR dues reassessed at ₹640 bn  
• ~20% lower vs earlier outstanding dues  
• No interest accrual improves liability economics  
• Effective AGR burden reduced on NPV basis  
• Majority dues payable over FY36–41  
• Regulatory uncertainty largely resolved  
• ₹250 bn debt raise now key trigger  
• ₹450 bn capex execution depends on funding

**CLSA on Vodafone Idea**

**Target Price: ₹11**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

AGR relief improves funding visibility  

• AGR dues cut to ₹640 bn from ₹877 bn  
• 10-year moratorium on AGR payments  
• Minimal payments from FY32–35  
• Larger instalments scheduled FY36–41  
• Spectrum debt remains high at ₹1,249 bn  
• Govt converted ₹370 bn debt to equity  
• Govt stake increased to ~49%  
• AGR clarity supports future fundraising plans

**Citi on JSPL**

**Target Price: ₹980 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Sell**  

Valuation rich despite improving operating metrics  

• Q4 EBITDA ~₹26.5 bn, up ~7% YoY  
• Volume growth strong at ~23% YoY  
• Realizations largely flat YoY  
• Costs increased ~3%, impacting margins  
• EBITDA/t at ~₹10,100 vs ₹11,650 YoY  
• Focus on utilization over value addition  
• Valuation at ~8.6x FY28 EV/EBITDA  
• Steel price correction poses downside risk

**Morgan Stanley on JSPL**

**Target Price: ₹1,250**  
**Recommendation: Overweight**  

Better realizations and volumes drive strong beat  

• EBITDA ₹29.4 bn, ~25% above estimates  
• Adjusted EBITDA ~13% above expectations  
• Volumes and realizations beat estimates  
• Opex/t lower than expected  
• Net debt increased to ₹160 bn  
• Net debt/EBITDA at ~1.66x  
• One-off impairment on Australia assets  
• FY27 outlook: higher realizations, rising coal costs

**CLSA on JSPL**

**Target Price: ₹1,420**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

Strong volume-led growth with robust outlook  

• Q4 EBITDA beat led by higher volumes  
• Volumes up 23% YoY in quarter  
• EBITDA/t improved to ₹10,103  
• Angul expansion to drive volume ramp-up  
• FY27 steel sales guided at 10.5–11 mt  
• Expect ~40% EBITDA CAGR FY26–28  
• Higher steel prices supporting near-term outlook  
• Capacity ramp-up execution remains key

**Jefferies on Avenue Supermarts**

**Target Price: ₹4,500 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Hold**  

Growth improving with expansion momentum  

• EBITDA grew ~25% YoY in Q4  
• LFL growth returned to double digits  
• Record store additions at 58 stores  
• Entered multiple new states in quarter  
• Temporary demand spike normalized by month-end  
• DMart Ready operations continue rationalisation

**Morgan Stanley on Avenue Supermarts**

**Target Price: ₹5,188**  
**Recommendation: Overweight**  

Strong LFL growth with steady margin improvement  

• Gross margin up 40 bps YoY  
• EBITDA margin improved 42 bps YoY  
• LFL growth at 10.8%, multi-quarter high  
• No supply chain disruptions observed  
• March demand spike normalized later

**CLSA on Avenue Supermarts**

**Target Price: ₹6,628**  
**Recommendation: Outperform**  

Strong operating leverage driving margin expansion  

• EBITDA grew 25% YoY in Q4  
• PAT growth stood at 17% YoY  
• Gross margins broadly in line estimates  
• EBITDA margin beat driven by cost leverage  
• Higher employee costs from store expansions  
• PAT lagged EBITDA due to lower other income

**Citi on Avenue Supermarts**

**Target Price: ₹3,650 (Raised)**  
**Recommendation: Sell**  

Improving demand but profitability and cash flow concerns  

• 4Q SSG improved to 10.8% QoQ  
• Revenue/PAT grew 19%/17% YoY  
• March demand spike normalized by month-end  
• Bill cuts per sq ft declined sequentially  
• Consolidated FCF negative at ₹9.6 bn  
• Net debt at ₹6.6 bn vs cash earlier  
• Profit lagging revenue in recent quarters  
• Competition and higher costs impacting margins
